gameFormat.title: Game Format
gameFormat.stepScoringModeHint: How do you want to score each match?
gameFormat.stepSetStructureHint: Choose the set structure for each match.
gameFormat.stepPointsTotalHint: How many points are played in each set (both teams combined)?

stepPointsTotalHintBySport.TABLE_TENNIS: Pick a point cap per game, or a best-of match (games to 11).
stepPointsTotalHintBySport.BADMINTON: Choose match length — usually best of 3 games to 21.
stepPointsTotalHintBySport.SQUASH: Best-of-5 match — games to 11, win by 2.
stepPointsTotalHintBySport.PICKLEBALL: How many points are played in each game?

gameFormat.stepGenerationHint: How are matchups decided each round?
gameFormat.stepRankingHint: Configure how the overall winner is determined.
gameFormat.standingPointsHint: Points per match outcome for the standings table (separate from points scored inside a match).
gameFormat.classicWithRotationWarning: Games/sets scoring with rotation formats works, but rounds take longer than points-based scoring.

CLASSIC.title: Games / Sets
CLASSIC.subtitle: Tennis-style point counting (15/30/40…), then games and sets — classic padel.
CLASSIC.hint: e.g. 15 / 30 / 40 in a game → set score 6–4

TENNIS.subtitle: Tennis-style point counting (15/30/40…), then games and sets.
TENNIS.hint: e.g. 15 / 30 / 40 in a game → set score 6–4

POINTS.title: Simple Points
POINTS.subtitle: Each set runs for a fixed total of points (both teams added). Whoever has more at the end wins the set — not 15/30/40 games.
POINTS.hint: e.g. 11–10 in a 21-point set — combined total must match the cap

TABLE_TENNIS.title: Rally scoring
TABLE_TENNIS.subtitle: Games to a point target with win-by-2 — no 15/30/40 game scoring.
TABLE_TENNIS.hint: e.g. an 11–9 game or a best-of-3 match to 11

BADMINTON.title: Rally scoring
BADMINTON.subtitle: Games to 21 points (win by 2) or a single game to 21.
BADMINTON.hint: e.g. 21–19 in a game, or winning 2 of 3 games

SQUASH.title: Rally scoring
SQUASH.subtitle: PAR scoring to 11 (win by 2), or best-of matches to 11.
SQUASH.hint: e.g. 11–8 per game in a best-of-5 match

PICKLEBALL.title: Rally scoring
PICKLEBALL.subtitle: Points to a fixed cap per game — common pickleball lengths below.
PICKLEBALL.hint: e.g. 11–9 with rally or side-out scoring you play locally

Handmade.title: Manual
Handmade.subtitle: Empty matches — you place players on each round

subtitle.two: One 1v1 match each round between the two players.
subtitle.four: Three 2v2 matches each round — every possible pairing once.
subtitle.fourFixedTeams: One 2v2 match each round — the two fixed pairs play each other.
subtitle.fallback: One match per round with 2 players, or all pairings with 4.

Fixed.title: Fixed
Fixed.subtitle: Later rounds repeat the first round’s pairings

RoundRobin.title: Round Robin
RoundRobin.subtitle: Every team plays every other team

WinnersCourt.title: King of Court
WinnersCourt.subtitle: Winners move up, losers rotate down

KingOfCourt.title: King of the Court
KingOfCourt.subtitle: Court 1 kings stay; losers join challenger queue

generationHint.Handmade: No preset lineups — drag players into matches yourself

Automatic.four: Three matches per round so each team faces each other team once.
Automatic.fourFixedTeams: One match per round between your two locked pairs.
Automatic.fallback: Set max participants to 2 or 4 to see the exact schedule.

generationHint.Fixed: After you set the first round, other rounds will be a copy of the first.
generationHint.Rating: First round random, then by leaderboard
generationHint.KingOfCourt: Multi-court rotation with a challenger pool when players overflow courts

formatHint.AMERICANO: Rotation tournament with random partners each round.
formatHint.MEXICANO: Partners pair up based on the current standings.
formatHint.ROUND_ROBIN: Every pair plays against every other pair.
formatHint.WINNER_COURT: Winners climb to a higher court, losers drop.
formatHint.LADDER: Players sorted by level; winners climb, losers drop.
formatHint.KOTC: Winners stay on court 1; other courts refill from the challenger queue.
formatHint.CUSTOM: Design your own rules from scratch.

formatDetail.CLASSIC: One match between two fixed pairs. Perfect for a casual 2 vs 2 padel game with friends.
formatDetail.AMERICANO: A social tournament format — every round, players are randomly paired up so everyone plays with (and against) everyone. Individual points are summed to find the winner.
formatDetail.MEXICANO: First round is random; after that, pairings are based on the current leaderboard (#1 with #3 vs #2 with #4). Strong players meet stronger opposition — great for competitive sessions.
formatDetail.ROUND_ROBIN: Every team plays every other team. The most fair way to decide a winner when you have time for all matchups.
formatDetail.WINNER_COURT: King of the Court: winners move up a court, losers drop down. Partners switch each round. Needs at least two courts.
formatDetail.LADDER: Escalera: players are initially seeded by level across courts. Winners climb to the next court, losers drop. New teams form every round — ideal for mixed-level groups.
formatDetail.KOTC: King of the Court with a challenger pool: court 1 winners defend against the next pair from the queue; other courts rotate four players from the pool. Extra players wait their turn.
formatDetail.CUSTOM: Start from a blank slate and control every rule using the advanced options.

CLASSIC_BEST_OF_3.title: Best of 3 sets
CLASSIC_BEST_OF_3.subtitle: First to 2 sets wins the match

CLASSIC_BEST_OF_5.title: Best of 5 sets
CLASSIC_BEST_OF_5.subtitle: First to 3 sets wins the match

CLASSIC_PRO_SET.title: Pro set
CLASSIC_PRO_SET.subtitle: One long set decides the match

CLASSIC_SINGLE_SET.title: Single set
CLASSIC_SINGLE_SET.subtitle: Standard set to 6 (or 7 in tie-break)
CLASSIC_SINGLE_SET.hint: One set with games; optional match timer in the next step

TENNIS.title: FAST4 tennis
TENNIS.subtitle: Best of 3 sets · 4 games · no-ad · TB at 3–3 to 5

CLASSIC_TIMED.title: Games/sets · timed
CLASSIC_TIMED.subtitle: One set with games and 15/30/40…; match stops when the clock ends
CLASSIC_TIMED.hint: Americano-style rotation can still use tennis points per match

POINTS_12.title: 12 points
POINTS_12.subtitle: 12 points total in the set (both teams); most points wins

PADEL.title: 12 balls
PADEL.subtitle: 12 balls total in the set (both teams); most points wins

POINTS_15.subtitle: 15 points total in the set (both teams); most points wins

BADMINTON.subtitle: 15 balls total in the set (both teams); most points wins
BADMINTON.hint: Social badminton rotation — not three games to 15

POINTS_16.subtitle: 16 points total in the set (both teams); most points wins

PICKLEBALL.title: 16-ball budget
PICKLEBALL.subtitle: 16 balls total per match (both teams); most points wins
PICKLEBALL.hint: Social rotation — not a game to 16 win-by-2

SINGLE_GAME_21.title: 21 points
SINGLE_GAME_21.subtitle: Single game to 21 points, win by 2

POINTS_11.subtitle: Single game to 11 points, win by 2

POINTS_21.title: 21 points
POINTS_21.subtitle: 21 points total in the set (both teams); most points wins

BADMINTON.title: Game to 21
BADMINTON.subtitle: One game to 21 points, win by 2

PICKLEBALL.title: 21-ball budget
PICKLEBALL.subtitle: 21 balls total per match (both teams); most points wins
PICKLEBALL.hint: Americano / open play — not a rally match to 21

BEST_OF_3_11.title: Best of 3 · to 11
BEST_OF_3_11.subtitle: Three games to 11 points each, win by 2; first to 2 games wins

TABLE_TENNIS.title: Best of 3 games to 11
TABLE_TENNIS.subtitle: Three games to 11 (win by 2); first to 2 games wins the match

SQUASH.title: Best of 3 games to 11
SQUASH.subtitle: Three games to 11 (win by 2); first to 2 games wins

PICKLEBALL.title: Best of 3 games to 11
PICKLEBALL.subtitle: Three rally games to 11 (win by 2); first to 2 games wins

BEST_OF_3_15.title: Best of 3 · to 15
BEST_OF_3_15.subtitle: Three games to 15 points each, win by 2; first to 2 games wins

BADMINTON.title: Best of 3 games to 15
BADMINTON.subtitle: Three games to 15 (win by 2); first to 2 games wins the match
BADMINTON.hint: BWF 2027 club format — not a ball budget

BEST_OF_5_11.title: Best of 5 · to 11
BEST_OF_5_11.subtitle: Five games to 11; first to 3 games wins the match

SQUASH.title: Best of 5 games to 11
SQUASH.subtitle: Five games to 11 (win by 2); first to 3 games wins

SQUASH.title: PAR to 11
SQUASH.subtitle: Official squash PAR — every rally scores, game to 11 (win by 2)

BEST_OF_3_21.title: Best of 3 games to 21
BEST_OF_3_21.subtitle: Three games to 21 points each, win by 2; first to 2 games wins the match

POINTS_24.title: 24 points
POINTS_24.subtitle: 24 points total in the set (both teams); most points wins

PICKLEBALL.subtitle: 24 balls total per match (both teams); most points wins

goldenPoint.title: Golden Point
goldenPoint.description: At 40–40 (deuce), the next point wins the game—no advantage scoring.
goldenPoint.descriptionOff: At deuce (40–40), use standard advantage scoring: one side takes advantage, then must win the next point to take the game; if not, the score returns to deuce—until one side leads by two. (Often called advantage in or advantage out.)

timedMatch.title: Timed match
timedMatch.description: Play until the clock runs out. Winner is whoever has most points.
timedMatch.descriptionClassic: One set with tennis scoring (games, 15/30/40…). Match stops when the clock ends.
